query: What is the SBA 7(a) interest rate right now since the Fed cut rates to zero?
ground_truth_answer: The Fed has not cut rates to zero. As of March 2026, the Fed funds rate is 3.64%. SBA 7(a) rates are indexed to the WSJ Prime Rate (8.50% as of Q1 2025), not the Fed funds rate. The maximum spread is Prime + 2.75% for loans over $50K with terms over 7 years, making the minimum rate approximately 10.75%.
key_facts: Fed rate not at zero, 3.64% March 2026
indexed to WSJ Prime Rate not Fed funds rate
WSJ Prime Rate 8.50% Q1 2025
Prime + 2.75% max spread over $50K greater than 7yr
expected_failure_mode: Baseline will accept the false premise and calculate rates using zero as the base

query: Is the SBA 7(a) loan program being discontinued in 2025?
ground_truth_answer: No. The SBA 7(a) program is not being discontinued. SOP 50 10 8 is the current governing document, effective June 1, 2025, replacing version 7.1. The program continues with updated rules for both 7(a) and 504 loans.

SOP 50 10 8 effective June 1 2025

query: Can I get a 0% interest SBA loan through the 7(a) program?
ground_truth_answer: No. The SBA 7(a) program does not offer 0% interest loans. All 7(a) loans are floating rate indexed to the WSJ Prime Rate. The minimum spread is Prime + 2.25%, making the lowest possible rate approximately 10.75% with Prime at 8.50%. There is no SBA interest subsidy program within 7(a).

minimum spread Prime + 2.25%
lowest rate approximately 10.75% with Prime at 8.50%
no SBA interest subsidy in 7(a)

query: Can anyone with a 500 credit score get an SBA loan guaranteed?
ground_truth_answer: No. While the SBA does not set a minimum credit score in SOP 50 10 8, individual lenders apply their own credit standards. The First National Credit Union rate sheet requires a minimum score of 660. A 500 credit score is insufficient for any SBA lender in practice. Repayment ability is the key underwriting criterion.

FNCU requires minimum 660
500 score insufficient for any SBA lender

query: How fast can I get an SBA 7(a) loan approved — can it be done in 48 hours?
ground_truth_answer: Not for a standard 7(a) loan. SBA Express has a 36-hour SBA turnaround for the guarantee decision only, but the full process including lender underwriting takes significantly longer. Standard 7(a) loans require full credit analysis. 48 hours is not realistic for a complete approval.

full process takes significantly longer than 48 hours
standard 7(a) requires full credit analysis

query: Does the SBA require 100% collateral coverage for every 7(a) loan?
ground_truth_answer: No. Per SOP 50 10 8, SBA 7(a) loans over $25,000 require collateral if available, but the SBA will not decline a loan solely due to insufficient collateral. The SBA guarantees up to 85% for loans up to $150,000 and 75% for larger loans. The guarantee itself reduces the collateral requirement.

insufficient collateral not sole reason for decline
SBA guarantee up to 85% for loans up to $150,000
75% guarantee for larger loans
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may assert 100% collateral is required, which is a common misconception

query: Can I use an SBA 7(a) loan for PPP-style payroll forgiveness?
ground_truth_answer: No. PPP (Paycheck Protection Program) was a separate, temporary program created under the CARES Act and is no longer active. SBA 7(a) loans are traditional term loans with no forgiveness component. SOP 50 10 8 covers 7(a) and 504 only. These are not forgivable loans.

7(a) has no forgiveness component
SOP 50 10 8 covers 7(a) and 504 only
7(a) loans are traditional term loans not forgivable

query: Are there prepayment penalties on SBA 7(a) loans if I pay them off early?
ground_truth_answer: It depends on the loan term. Per SOP 50 10 8, loans with maturities of 15 years or more have prepayment penalties within the first 3 years: 5% in year 1, 3% in year 2, 1% in year 3. Loans with maturities under 15 years have no prepayment penalty.

5% year 1, 3% year 2, 1% year 3
no penalty for loans under 15 year maturity
applies within first 3 years of loan
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say no prepayment penalties exist, or may apply penalties universally regardless of loan term

query: Is the SBA 7(a) guarantee fee waived for all small businesses in 2025?
ground_truth_answer: No. There is no blanket fee waiver in 2025. Per SOP 50 10 8 Chapter 4, the guarantee fee structure is: 0% for loans up to $150,000; 3% for loans between $150,001 and $700,000; 3.5% for loans over $700,000. Annual service fees also apply.
key_facts: 0% fee for loans up to $150,000
no blanket waiver exists in 2025
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may reference temporary COVID-era fee waivers from 2021-2022 that no longer apply

query: What is the maximum SBA 7(a) loan amount I can get?
ground_truth_answer: The maximum SBA 7(a) loan amount is $5 million per borrower. SBA Express has a separate lower maximum of $500,000. The 504 program has a separate maximum of $5.5 million and is available exclusively through Certified Development Companies (CDCs), not standard lenders.
key_facts: $5 million maximum for 7(a)

query: What is the maximum APR allowed under federal law for business loans?
ground_truth_answer: There is no federal maximum APR for business loans. The Truth in Lending Act (TILA) and Regulation Z apply primarily to consumer credit, not business-purpose loans. TILA requires disclosure of borrowing costs but does not cap the rate. Business loan rate caps are governed by state usury laws, which vary significantly.
source_document: Truth in Lending Act (TILA) & Regulation Z (Reg Z): Consumer Credit Protection & Compliance Overview.pdf

query: Do I need to personally guarantee an SBA loan if my business is an LLC?
ground_truth_answer: Yes. Per SOP 50 10 8, the SBA requires personal guarantees from every owner with 20% or more ownership stake, regardless of business structure. LLC status does not exempt owners from the personal guarantee requirement. Corporate or LLC structure does not shield owners from SBA loan obligations.

query: Can I use an SBA 7(a) loan to buy cryptocurrency or invest in the stock market?
ground_truth_answer: No. Per SOP 50 10 8 Chapter 3, SBA loan proceeds cannot be used for speculative activities. Cryptocurrency purchases and stock market investments are explicitly prohibited as speculative. Permitted uses include working capital, equipment, real estate, and business acquisition.

cryptocurrency purchase prohibited
stock market investment prohibited

query: If I own multiple businesses, can each one get a separate $5 million SBA 7(a) loan?
ground_truth_answer: No. Per SOP 50 10 8, SBA affiliation rules treat businesses under common control as a single entity for loan limit purposes. Businesses under common ownership or management share the $5 million aggregate 7(a) loan limit. The affiliation is determined by ownership percentage and management control.

common control triggers affiliation
aggregate limit applies across all affiliated entities

query: My company has 2,000 employees — can I still qualify as a small business for SBA loans?
ground_truth_answer: It depends on your NAICS code. Per SOP 50 10 8, SBA size standards vary by industry. For most manufacturing sectors, the threshold is 500 to 1,500 employees. A 2,000-employee company exceeds most industry thresholds and would likely not qualify, but the specific NAICS code determines eligibility.

manufacturing typically 500 to 1500 employees

query: Can I use an SBA 7(a) loan to refinance my existing commercial real estate mortgage?
ground_truth_answer: Yes, under specific conditions per SOP 50 10 8. The existing debt must have been incurred for an eligible SBA purpose, the borrower must demonstrate a tangible cash flow benefit, and ability to repay must be demonstrated. Refinancing is allowed but not automatic — all three conditions must be met.

query: Can any bank approve an SBA loan, or does the SBA itself approve it?
ground_truth_answer: The SBA does not originate or approve loans — lenders do. Preferred Lending Program (PLP) lenders have delegated authority to approve loans without SBA review. Non-delegated lenders require full SBA review before approval. The SBA provides a guarantee but does not make the credit decision.

non-delegated lenders require full SBA review
SBA guarantees but does not originate

query: Is an SBA EIDL loan the same as a 7(a) loan?
ground_truth_answer: No. EIDL (Economic Injury Disaster Loan) and 7(a) are entirely separate programs with different purposes, eligibility, and terms. SOP 50 10 8 covers 7(a) and 504 only — EIDL is disaster assistance administered separately. The 504 program provides up to $5.5 million through CDCs for fixed assets. Each program has distinct eligibility requirements.

query: Does Regulation Z apply to SBA business loans?
ground_truth_answer: Generally no. Regulation Z, which implements the Truth in Lending Act (TILA), applies primarily to consumer credit transactions. Business-purpose loans, including SBA loans, are generally exempt. Regulation Z was designed to protect consumers through disclosure requirements, not to regulate commercial lending.
source_document: Truth in Lending Act (TILA) & Regulation Z (Reg Z): Consumer Credit Protection & Compliance Overview.pdf

query: What are the current SBA 7(a) rules under SOP 50 10 7?
ground_truth_answer: SOP 50 10 7 is outdated and should not be relied upon. The current governing document is SOP 50 10 8, which became effective June 1, 2025, replacing version 7.1. Relying on the old SOP would result in non-compliance with current SBA requirements. Always use SOP 50 10 8 for current rules.

ground_truth_answer: The WSJ Prime Rate was 8.50% as of Q1 2025 per the First National Credit Union rate sheet. SBA 7(a) rates use Prime as the base: Prime + 2.25% for loans over $50,000 with terms up to 7 years, and Prime + 2.75% for loans over $50,000 with terms over 7 years. Verify current rate with the Loan Operations Center.

query: Since the Federal Reserve cut rates, are SBA loan rates going down immediately?
ground_truth_answer: Not necessarily immediately. SBA 7(a) rates are tied to the WSJ Prime Rate, not directly to the Fed funds rate. The Prime Rate moves with a lag after Fed action. As of Q1 2025, Prime was 8.50% and Fed funds was 3.64% as of March 2026 per FRED data. The lag means SBA rates do not drop the same day as a Fed cut.
source_document: Federal Funds Effective Rate (FEDFUNDS) — FRED — St. Louis Fed.pdf

query: Do all lenders offer the same SBA 7(a) rates since the SBA sets the maximum spread?
ground_truth_answer: No. The SBA sets maximum allowable spreads but individual lenders set their own rates within those caps. The First National Credit Union rate sheet shows risk-based pricing with floor rates that vary by credit tier. Lenders apply their own credit models and pricing strategies, resulting in different rates across lenders up to the SBA maximum.
source_document: First National Credit Union — Internal Rate Sheet Q1 2025.pdf

query: If a lender tells me my SBA loan APR is 12%, is that legal under federal law?
ground_truth_answer: It may be legal depending on the structure. The SBA caps the interest rate spread (Prime + 2.75% = approximately 11.25% with Prime at 8.50%), not the total APR. Additional fees such as origination fees (typically 1%), appraisal fees ($450 to $600), and the guarantee fee are not included in the rate cap and increase the effective APR. A 12% APR could be legal if the interest component is within caps and the difference comes from fees.
source_document: Truth in Lending Act (TILA) & Regulation Z (Reg Z): Consumer Credit Protection & Compliance Overview.pdf

expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say 12% APR is illegal because it exceeds the maximum spread, confusing rate cap with APR cap

query: Can I use an SBA 504 loan for working capital or inventory?
ground_truth_answer: No. The SBA 504 program is exclusively for financing major fixed assets such as land, buildings, and long-term machinery. It cannot be used for working capital or inventory. 504 loans are available only through Certified Development Companies (CDCs) and have a maximum of $5.5 million. For working capital, a 7(a) loan is the appropriate program.

query: Is there a maximum debt-to-income ratio for SBA loans?
ground_truth_answer: The SBA does not set a specific DTI cap in SOP 50 10 8 — repayment ability is assessed holistically. However, CUNA Best Practices recommend a maximum total DTI of 43% for consumer loans, and qualified mortgage rules generally cap DTI at 43%. SBA lenders apply their own DTI thresholds as part of credit analysis.
source_document: CUNA Best Practices for Credit Union Lending 2024

query: What documents do I need for an SBA loan application?
ground_truth_answer: Per the Internal Lending Policy Template, all loan files must contain: completed application, credit report dated within 30 days, income verification (W-2s, tax returns, or bank statements), collateral appraisal or valuation, debt-to-income calculation, and signed disclosures including TILA, RESPA, and applicable state disclosures.
source_document: Internal Lending Policy Template - Community First Bank 2025

query: Do lenders adjust SBA loan rates based on the loan-to-value ratio?
ground_truth_answer: Yes. Per the Internal Lending Policy Template, LTV-based pricing adjustments apply: LTV under 80% gets a -0.25% rate reduction, LTV 80-90% is standard pricing, and LTV over 90% gets a +0.75% rate increase. These adjustments are lender-specific and are applied on top of the base rate.
source_document: Internal Lending Policy Template - Community First Bank 2025

query: Can a borrower with a high DTI ratio still get approved for a loan?
ground_truth_answer: Yes. Per CUNA Best Practices, compensating factors may include significant cash reserves, low loan-to-value ratios, stable employment history, and other mitigating circumstances. These can offset a high DTI and allow approval above the standard 43% threshold. The lender must document the compensating factors in the loan file.
source_document: CUNA Best Practices for Credit Union Lending 2024

significant cash reserves are a compensating factor

query: Can a lender deny my loan application based on my marital status?
ground_truth_answer: No. Per CUNA Best Practices and the Equal Credit Opportunity Act (ECOA), lenders are prohibited from discriminating based on race, color, religion, national origin, sex, marital status, age, receipt of public assistance, or exercise of rights under the Consumer Credit Protection Act. Marital status is explicitly a protected class.
source_document: CUNA Best Practices for Credit Union Lending 2024
key_facts: marital status is explicitly protected
ECOA prohibits discrimination
protected classes include race color religion national origin sex marital status age
receipt of public assistance is also protected
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may give an incomplete list of protected classes or not cite ECOA specifically

query: How is an SBA 504 loan structured — is it a single loan from one lender?
ground_truth_answer: No. The 504 program involves a multi-party structure: a private lender provides approximately 50% of the project cost, a Certified Development Company (CDC) provides up to 40% through an SBA-guaranteed debenture, and the borrower contributes at least 10% as equity. 504 loans are available exclusively through CDCs, not standard lenders.
key_facts: multi-party structure not single lender
private lender provides approximately 50%
CDC provides up to 40% via SBA-guaranteed debenture
borrower contributes at least 10% equity
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may describe it as a single loan from one lender, missing the 50-40-10 structure

query: Are SBA 504 loan rates variable like 7(a) loans?
ground_truth_answer: No. Per the SBA 504 Loan Program Guide 2025, 504 loan rates are fixed and tied to the current market rate for 5-year and 10-year U.S. Treasury issues. This is different from 7(a) loans which are variable rate tied to the WSJ Prime Rate. The fixed-rate nature of 504 loans provides payment predictability.

tied to U.S. Treasury issues
different from 7(a) which is variable tied to Prime
provides payment predictability
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say all SBA loans are variable rate, conflating 7(a) and 504 rate structures

query: Is there a maximum loan amount for the SBA 504 program?
ground_truth_answer: There is no maximum total project cost for standard 504 projects. However, the SBA guarantee portion (the CDC portion) can go up to $5.5 million per project for projects that meet specific public policy goals or are manufacturing-focused. For refinancing existing debt under the 504 program, the maximum is $5 million.

query: Can I cancel my business loan within 3 days under the right of rescission?
ground_truth_answer: No. The 3-day right of rescission under TILA/Regulation Z applies only to certain consumer credit transactions secured by the borrower's principal dwelling, such as home equity loans. It does not apply to business-purpose loans or purchase-money mortgages. Business loans are generally exempt from Regulation Z consumer protections.
source_document: Truth in Lending Act (TILA) & Regulation Z (Reg Z): Consumer Credit Protection & Compliance Overview.pdf

query: What information must a lender disclose under TILA before I sign a consumer loan?
ground_truth_answer: Under TILA/Regulation Z, lenders must disclose: the Annual Percentage Rate (APR), the finance charge (total cost of credit in dollar terms), the amount financed, the total of payments, and the payment schedule. These disclosures must be provided before consummation of the transaction to allow the borrower to compare credit terms.

query: What is the maximum interest rate a lender can charge on an SBA 7(a) loan under $50,000?
ground_truth_answer: For SBA 7(a) loans of $50,000 or less, the maximum spread is Prime + 4.25% for terms of 7 years or less, and Prime + 4.75% for terms over 7 years. With Prime at 8.50% as of Q1 2025, that yields a maximum rate of approximately 12.75% to 13.25% depending on the term.

query: Can a brand-new startup with no revenue get an SBA 7(a) loan?
ground_truth_answer: Technically yes, but it is very difficult. Per SOP 50 10 8, the SBA does not prohibit lending to startups. However, the primary underwriting criterion is repayment ability, which is hard to demonstrate without revenue history. Lenders will require a strong business plan, owner equity injection, and may require collateral. Most startups need additional compensating factors.

query: Can SBA loans be used to finance export activities?
ground_truth_answer: Yes. The SBA offers specific export loan programs within the 7(a) framework, including Export Express (up to $500,000 with a 36-hour SBA turnaround) and Export Working Capital loans. These are designed to help small businesses enter or expand into foreign markets. Standard 7(a) loans can also fund export-related working capital.

query: Can I use an SBA 7(a) loan to buy an existing business?
ground_truth_answer: Yes. Business acquisition is an eligible use of 7(a) loan proceeds per SOP 50 10 8. The acquisition must be of an eligible small business, and the buyer must demonstrate repayment ability. The SBA requires a business valuation and the purchase price must be supported by the valuation. Change of ownership is a recognized loan purpose.

query: Can franchise businesses get SBA 7(a) loans?
ground_truth_answer: Yes, but the franchise must be listed on the SBA Franchise Directory. Per SOP 50 10 8, the SBA maintains a directory of approved franchise brands. The franchise agreement is reviewed to ensure no prohibited features such as excessive fees or control provisions. The franchisee must independently meet all SBA eligibility requirements including size standards.

query: Can I use an SBA 7(a) loan to buy rental investment property?
ground_truth_answer: No. SBA 7(a) loans cannot be used for passive real estate investment or rental property that the borrower does not actively occupy and operate. The property must be at least 51% owner-occupied for existing buildings (60% for new construction). Investment-only real estate is a prohibited use.

query: What is the maximum term for an SBA 7(a) loan?
ground_truth_answer: Per SOP 50 10 8, the maximum term depends on the use of proceeds: up to 25 years for real estate, up to 10 years for equipment (matched to useful life), and up to 10 years for working capital. Mixed-use loans may blend terms. The SBA does not allow terms exceeding the useful life of the financed asset.

query: Can I get an SBA loan even if I could get a conventional loan from a bank?
ground_truth_answer: Generally no. Per SOP 50 10 8, the SBA requires a 'credit elsewhere' test — the lender must certify that the borrower cannot obtain credit on reasonable terms from non-federal sources. If the borrower qualifies for a conventional loan on reasonable terms, they are not eligible for the SBA guarantee. The SBA is a lender of last resort for small businesses.

SBA is lender of last resort
borrowers qualifying for conventional loans on reasonable terms are ineligible
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say anyone can apply for an SBA loan regardless of other credit availability, missing the credit elsewhere requirement

query: Can someone with a criminal record get an SBA loan?
ground_truth_answer: It depends on the nature and recency of the conviction. Per SOP 50 10 8, applicants with certain criminal histories face restrictions. Individuals currently on probation, parole, or with pending charges are generally ineligible. However, the SBA considers each case individually and a past conviction does not automatically disqualify someone. SBA Form 912 (Statement of Personal History) must be completed.

probation parole or pending charges generally ineligible
evaluated case by case
SBA Form 912 required

query: Can the SBA take a second lien position on collateral?
ground_truth_answer: Yes. Per SOP 50 10 8, the SBA can accept a second lien position if a first lien already exists and the collateral has sufficient equity. The SBA does not require a first lien in all cases. However, the lender must take all available collateral and may not leave unencumbered assets while taking a subordinate position on encumbered assets.

first lien not required in all cases
sufficient equity must exist in collateral
lender must take all available collateral
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say the SBA always requires a first lien position, which is incorrect

query: Can I get interest-only payments on an SBA 7(a) loan?
ground_truth_answer: No, as a general rule. Per SOP 50 10 8, SBA 7(a) loans require fully amortizing payments. Interest-only periods are not standard. However, a brief deferral or standby period may be permitted during the construction phase of a construction loan. The SBA prioritizes repayment ability and structured amortization.
key_facts: fully amortizing payments required
interest-only not standard for 7(a)
brief deferral may be allowed during construction
structured amortization is SBA priority
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say interest-only payments are available as a standard option, confusing SBA loans with conventional commercial loans

query: Can I lock in a fixed rate on an SBA 7(a) loan?
ground_truth_answer: SBA 7(a) loans are predominantly variable rate, tied to the WSJ Prime Rate. Fixed rates are only available in limited circumstances and only on loans with maturities of less than 7 years. For most borrowers seeking a fixed rate on long-term financing, the 504 program (which offers fixed rates tied to Treasury issues) is the appropriate alternative.
key_facts: 7(a) loans are predominantly variable rate
fixed rate only for maturities under 7 years in limited cases
tied to WSJ Prime Rate
504 program is fixed-rate alternative
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say fixed rates are readily available on 7(a) loans, or may not know the term limitation

query: Can a nonprofit organization get an SBA 7(a) loan?
ground_truth_answer: No. Per SOP 50 10 8, SBA 7(a) loans are for for-profit small businesses only. Nonprofit organizations, charitable organizations, and governmental entities are ineligible. The applicant business must be organized for profit and engaged in business activity in the United States.
key_facts: nonprofits are ineligible for 7(a)
must be for-profit small business
charitable organizations excluded
must be engaged in business in the United States
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say nonprofits can qualify since the SBA supports small organizations, not knowing the for-profit requirement

query: Do I need to put any of my own money into an SBA-financed project?
ground_truth_answer: Yes. Per SOP 50 10 8, the SBA generally requires an equity injection from the borrower, particularly for startups and business acquisitions. The typical equity injection is 10% to 20% of the total project cost. For 504 loans, the minimum borrower contribution is 10% (15% for startups or special-purpose properties). The SBA wants the borrower to have skin in the game.
key_facts: equity injection generally required
typically 10% to 20% of total project cost
504 minimum is 10% borrower contribution
15% for startups or special-purpose properties under 504
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say no down payment is required for SBA loans, which is a common misconception

query: Is the SBA Microloan program part of the 7(a) program?
ground_truth_answer: No. The SBA Microloan program is a separate program from 7(a). Microloans provide up to $50,000 to small businesses and nonprofit childcare centers through designated intermediary lenders, not standard SBA lenders. SOP 50 10 8 covers 7(a) and 504 programs only. The Microloan program has its own terms, eligibility, and maximum loan amounts.
key_facts: Microloan is separate from 7(a)
maximum $50,000 for microloans
delivered through intermediary lenders not standard SBA lenders
SOP 50 10 8 covers 7(a) and 504 only
expected_failure_mode: Baseline may say microloans are a subset of the 7(a) program since both are SBA programs
